hero.headline: Huisje 56
hero.subheadline: Your own terrace on the water, views across Lake Gaastmeer, and the freedom of car-free Recreatiepark de Liuwe Daem.

intro.heading: Welcome to Huisje 56
intro.body: Welcome to Huisje 56, our charming holiday cottage on the waterfront in Gaastmeer, Friesland. Step onto your private terrace, watch the boats drift by, and feel the pace of life slow down. The park is car-free, the lake is at your doorstep, and the Frisian countryside stretches out in every direction. This is where your holiday begins.

jetty.title: Private jetty
jetty.desc: Sail directly onto Lake Gaastmeer from your own jetty. Mooring up to approximately 10 metres.

carFree.title: Car-free park
carFree.desc: Your car stays safely in the car park. Bring luggage to the cottage by trolley — blissful peace and quiet.

guests.title: Up to 6 guests
guests.desc: Four bedrooms across two floors. Space for the whole family.

location.title: Waterfront location
location.desc: Decked terrace with panoramic views across Lake Gaastmeer and the iconic pyramid-shaped cottages.

photoGrid.heading: A glimpse inside
photoGrid.subheading: From the waterfront terrace to the cosy bedrooms

park.heading: Recreatiepark de Liuwe Daem
park.body: Liuwedaem is a small-scale park with 56 characteristic pyramid-shaped cottages, right on the shores of the Great Gaastmeer. Since 1974, families have been enjoying peace, water and space here. The park is car-free — you park at the car park and bring your belongings to the cottage by trolley. It takes a little getting used to, but it creates wonderful tranquility. Children can run around freely, and you can hear only the water and the birds.
features: Open grounds — no hedges or fences
Own parking space at the entrance

items.quote: The view across the lake was breathtaking. The kids played on the terrace and went out on the water all week. Absolutely coming back!
items.author: The Van der Berg family

items.quote: Such peace and quiet! The car-free park really makes a difference. We had lovely dinners on the terrace with views across the water.
items.author: Marieke & Pieter

items.quote: Perfect for a family week. Plenty of room for everyone, private jetty and the village of Gaastmeer within walking distance. Highly recommended!
items.author: The Smits family

cottage.heading: The Cottage
cottage.subheading: Detached A-frame pyramid cottage directly on the water
cottage.intro: Huisje 56 is a detached pyramid-shaped cottage with a wooden frame and pitched roof, built in 1974 and modernised over the years. With approximately 70 m² of living space and a plot of 85 m², it comfortably accommodates up to 6 persons. The generous decked terrace directly on the water makes every morning a delight.

entrance.name: Entrance / hallway
entrance.desc: Spacious entrance with ample storage for coats and shoes.

livingRoom.name: Living room (approx. 24 m²)
livingRoom.desc: Light, open living room with red sofa bed, exposed wooden beams and sliding doors leading to the waterfront terrace. The perfect spot for evenings together.

kitchen.name: Open kitchen
kitchen.desc: Fully equipped open kitchen, directly connected to the living room.

bedroom1.name: Bedroom 1 (ground floor)
bedroom1.desc: Ground floor bedroom with double bed. Ideal for guests who prefer to avoid the stairs.

bathroom.name: Bathroom
bathroom.desc: Bathroom with walk-in shower, washbasin and a designer heated towel rail.

landing.name: Landing with mezzanine
landing.desc: Airy landing with open mezzanine — the typical feature of a pyramid cottage.

bedroom2.name: Bedroom 2
bedroom2.desc: 2 single beds under the sloped roof with skylight.

bedroom3.name: Bedroom 3
bedroom3.desc: 2 single beds with blue and light blue duvets under skylight.

bedroom4.name: Bedroom 4
bedroom4.desc: Double bed with blue gingham duvet under sloped wood-panelled ceiling.

washbasin.name: Washbasin
washbasin.desc: Separate washbasin on the landing with mirror cabinet — handy for multiple guests.

terrace.name: Decked terrace
terrace.desc: Generous decked terrace directly on the water with dining table and chairs. Panoramic views across the lake and the iconic pyramid-shaped cottages.

jetty.name: Private jetty
jetty.desc: Mooring for approximately 10 metres. Water depth approximately 1.5 metres. Sail directly onto Lake Gaastmeer, Ringwiel, Zandmeer and the Fluessen.

gallery.heading: Photo gallery
gallery.subheading: See the cottage inside and out

area.heading: The Area
area.subheading: Water, nature and Frisian charm on your doorstep
area.intro: Gaastmeer is situated in the heart of the Frisian Lakes — one of the most beautiful waterway regions in the Netherlands. From your own jetty you sail directly onto Lake Gaastmeer and from there to dozens of other lakes, pools and waterways. But there is plenty to do on land too: picturesque villages, historic towns and endless cycling routes through the Frisian landscape.

watersports.heading: Water & Sailing
watersports.body: With the Gaastmeer lakes right at your door, the Oudegaster Brekken around the corner, and the Heegermeer and Fluessen — a magnificent sailing area — within reach. Whisper-boat routes have expanded greatly in recent years, ideal for short trips. You can hire boats in the village.
activities: Hire a whisper boat in Gaastmeer
Open waterway connection to the entire Frisian lake district
Ice skating in winter (on the lakes!)

cycling.heading: Cycling & Walking
cycling.body: The area is ideal for cycling through the typical Frisian polder landscape. The route via It Heidenskip to Workum is an absolute must — gorgeous views, quiet roads and lovely villages along the way.
activities: Cycling route via Heidenskip to Workum
Cycling and foot ferries across the lakes

towns.heading: Villages & Towns
towns.intro: Gaastmeer has only about 300 inhabitants — ultimate peace. But around it lie beautiful villages and historic towns just a few minutes' drive away.

gaastmeer.name: Gaastmeer
gaastmeer.desc: Voted Most Beautiful Village in Friesland in 2016. Small supermarket, fish & chips stall, boat hire and pleasant terraces.

heeg.name: Heeg
heeg.desc: The ultimate watersports village. Plenty of shops, restaurants and a lively harbour.

sneek.name: Sneek
sneek.desc: Historic Frisian city with a lovely town centre, good shops and interesting museums.

workum.name: Workum
workum.desc: Charming artists' town with the Jopie Huisman Museum — an absolute must.

bolsward.name: Bolsward
bolsward.desc: Historic Frisian town with a stunning town hall and the Martini Church.

leeuwarden.name: Leeuwarden
leeuwarden.desc: Capital of Friesland. Museums, shops and a lively city centre.

booking.heading: Prices & Book
booking.subheading: Check availability and send your enquiry

rates.heading: Rates on request
rates.note: We rent Huisje 56 directly — no booking fees. Rates depend on the period and length of stay and we are happy to discuss them with you. Send us a booking enquiry via the form below and we will share a quote as soon as possible.

calendar.heading: Availability calendar
calendar.instruction: Click an arrival date, then a departure date

form.heading: Booking enquiry
form.subheading: Fill in the form and we will contact you as soon as possible
form.name: Name *
form.success: Thank you for your enquiry! We will be in touch within 24 hours.

contact.heading: Contact
contact.subheading: Have a question or want to know more? We are happy to help.
contact.intro: Have a question about the cottage, the area or availability? Feel free to contact us via the form or send an email. We usually respond within one working day.

form.name: Name *
form.subject: Subject *
form.success: Thank you! We will be in touch as soon as possible.
form.error: Something went wrong. Please email info@huisje56.nl

route.heading: Directions
route.intro: On arrival, park in the car park at the entrance. You will find trolleys there to bring your luggage to the cottage — typical of car-free Liuwedaem!
route.byCarAmsterdam: From Amsterdam (~1.5 hrs): A7 towards Leeuwarden, exit Sneek-West, via Heeg to Gaastmeer.
route.byCarGermany: From Germany: A31 or A7 towards the Netherlands, via Heerenveen towards Sneek, then to Gaastmeer.
route.gps: GPS: Liuwedaem 56, Gaastmeer (then follow signs to Liuwedaem)

cookie.message: We use cookies to improve your experience on our website. Google Maps will only load after your consent.
